build.notes: Build completed successfully with only expected performance warnings (asset size warnings for js/editor.js 337 KiB and js/screen.js 373 KiB). No TypeScript errors or compilation issues.

lint.notes: CallToAction block has only 2 expected experimental API warnings (__experimentalToggleGroupControl and __experimentalToggleGroupControlOption) which are theme-wide and acceptable. No errors in the CallToAction block itself.

registration.notes: block.json is properly configured with apiVersion 3, correct name format (takt/call-to-action), all required attributes defined with correct types and defaults. All required files exist (block.json, index.tsx, CallToAction.tsx, CallToAction.php, CallToAction.svg).

attributes.notes: All attributes defined correctly in block.json: anchor (string), backgroundType (string enum: color|image, default: color), image (object with id and focalPoint), eyebrow (string), heading (string), description (string), buttons (array). Attributes are properly used in both TSX and PHP files.

edgeCases.notes: Tested 10 comprehensive scenarios on test page: 1) Full content with color background, 2) Full content with image background, 3) Minimal content (heading only) with color, 4) Minimal content with image, 5) Long content edge case, 6) Long content with image background, 7) Empty optional fields (heading only), 8) Single button, 9) Custom focal point, 10) No heading (description and buttons only). All scenarios render correctly. ThemeHeading properly hides empty fields. Long content wraps correctly within max-w-2xl constraint. Single button renders properly. No heading scenario correctly omits aria-labelledby.

interactiveBehavior.reason: No interactive elements - block is static with heading, description, and button links only

accessibility.notes: Semantic HTML verified: Uses <section> element with proper structure. aria-labelledby correctly applied when heading exists (9 of 10 blocks have headings and aria-labelledby, 1 block without heading correctly has no aria-labelledby). Background images are properly marked as decorative with alt='' and aria-hidden='true' (verified in PHP code lines 28-29). Buttons are proper <a> elements with accessible labels. Heading hierarchy is correct (h2 elements). Dark mode gradient overlay on image backgrounds provides sufficient contrast. No keyboard navigation issues (buttons are standard links).
accessibility.patternCompliance: Not applicable - block uses standard semantic HTML without specific WAI-ARIA patterns like tabs/accordion/carousel

markupDuplication.notes: No markup duplication detected. Single DOM structure with responsive classes (max-sm:flex-col) used for layout adaptation. No JS-based content cloning.

issues.description: Missing @var docblock at top of CallToAction.php file
issues.fix: Add docblock with @var annotations for camelCase variables (backgroundType, eyebrow, heading, description, buttons, image) to prevent PHP_CodeSniffer warnings

tsxPhpSync.notes: TSX and PHP produce identical HTML structure: same <section> wrapper, same class names and conditional classes (bg-accent for color, dark class for image with gradient overlay), same container structure, same element order, same conditional rendering (image only when isDark && hasImage, ThemeHeading with same props). No manual attribute extraction detected (verified no $attributes['key'] pattern in PHP). The only issue is the missing docblock which is a coding standards issue, not a functional issue.

innerBlocks.reason: Block does not use inner blocks

specCompliance.notes: Test page (https://ign.localhost/test-call-to-action/, page ID 42) contains all 10 required test scenarios from the planning spec, properly wrapped in DemoContainer blocks. All scenarios use real media IDs (18, 19) not placeholders. Block count verified: 10 takt/call-to-action blocks found. All attributes match spec requirements. Accessibility fix successfully applied: background images have alt='' and aria-hidden='true'.

issues.description: Missing @var docblock at top of CallToAction.php file for camelCase variables
issues.fix:
Add docblock before line 1 with @var annotations:

<?php
/**
 * Call to Action block frontend template.
 *
 * Available variables (auto-extracted from $attributes):
 * @var string $anchor
 * @var string $backgroundType
 * @var array $image
 * @var string $eyebrow
 * @var string $heading
 * @var string $description
 * @var array $buttons
 *
 * Also available:
 * @var string $children Inner blocks content (not used)
 * @var WP_Block $block Block instance
 */
?>
issues.reasoning: The @var docblock tells PHP_CodeSniffer that camelCase variables like $backgroundType, $eyebrow come from block attributes (which use camelCase from JavaScript) and should be ignored. Without the docblock, every use of these variables triggers a PHP_CodeSniffer warning because WordPress coding standards prefer snake_case.

passedChecks: Block registers correctly with proper block.json configuration
All attributes defined with correct types and defaults
All 10 test scenarios render correctly on test page
Edge cases handled properly: empty fields hidden, long content wraps, single button works
Semantic HTML: proper <section> elements, h2 headings, proper button links
Accessibility fix verified: background images have alt='' (empty alt text)
aria-labelledby correctly applied when heading exists, omitted when no heading
Color background variant uses bg-accent class correctly
Image background variant applies dark mode with gradient overlay correctly
Focal point positioning works (object-position CSS applied)
ThemeHeading component renders conditionally (empty fields hidden)
Buttons render with correct variations (primary, secondary)
Responsive layout works (buttons stack on mobile via max-sm:flex-col)
TSX/PHP structure matches (same elements, classes, conditionals, order)
No manual attribute extraction in PHP (uses auto-extracted variables)
Test page uses DemoContainer wrappers correctly

scenarios.title: Color Background - Full Content
scenarios.notes: All fields render: eyebrow, heading, description, 2 buttons (primary + secondary). bg-accent class applied.

scenarios.title: Image Background - Full Content
scenarios.notes: All fields render with image background. Dark mode with gradient overlay applied. Background image has alt='' as expected. 2 buttons render correctly.

scenarios.title: Color Background - Minimal Content
scenarios.notes: Only heading renders. Empty eyebrow, description, and buttons are properly hidden. No empty containers.

scenarios.title: Image Background - Minimal Content
scenarios.notes: Heading and image background only. Empty fields hidden. Dark mode with gradient applied correctly.

scenarios.title: Long Content Edge Case
scenarios.notes: Very long heading (166 chars), long eyebrow, long description, and buttons with long titles all wrap correctly within max-w-2xl constraint. No overflow issues.

scenarios.title: Long Content - Image Background
scenarios.notes: Long content with image background. Text readability maintained over image with gradient overlay. Layout remains stable with taller content.

scenarios.title: Empty Optional Fields
scenarios.notes: Heading only. All optional fields empty and properly hidden. ThemeHeading conditional rendering works correctly.

scenarios.title: Single Button
scenarios.notes: Single primary button renders correctly. Button container layout works with one item. max-sm:flex-col class applied.

scenarios.title: Image Background - Custom Focal Point
scenarios.notes: Focal point (x: 0.5, y: 0.2) correctly applies object-position CSS. Image focuses on top portion as expected.

scenarios.title: No Heading - Description and Buttons Only
scenarios.notes: Description and 2 buttons render without heading. aria-labelledby correctly omitted (no heading to reference). Graceful handling of this edge case.

productionReadiness.notes: Block is production-ready. The single minor issue (missing docblock) is a coding standards issue that does not affect functionality. The accessibility fix has been successfully applied and verified.
